title: Wayland layer-shell overlay surface
notes: Overlay layer surface for app launcher popup
prompt: In crates/thermal-launch/src/main.rs, implement a Wayland layer-shell overlay surface using smithay-client-toolkit and wayland-client. The surface must: (1) connect to the Wayland display and enumerate globals, (2) bind wlr-layer-shell (wayland-protocols-wlr) and create a layer surface on the OVERLAY layer with keyboard_interactivity set to exclusive, (3) set anchor to center with no margin, and a fixed size of 700x500, (4) commit the surface and enter a basic event loop via wayland-client's EventQueue. Import thermal_core::ThermalPalette for color constants. Add a LauncherSurface struct holding the wl_surface, layer_surface, and a bool `configured`. Handle the layer_surface configure event to mark configured=true and ack the serial. The event loop should exit on Escape key or close. No rendering yet — just the surface plumbing. Dependencies available: smithay-client-toolkit = '0.19', wayland-client = '0.31', wayland-protocols-wlr = '0.3', thermal-core (local). Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass with no errors.

title: wgpu rendering pipeline
notes: Full-screen overlay with dark thermal background. Blocked on task 1 (needs configured Wayland surface).
prompt: In crates/thermal-launch/src/main.rs, add a wgpu rendering pipeline to the LauncherSurface established in task 1. Steps: (1) create wgpu::Instance with Vulkan/GL backends, (2) create a wgpu surface from the wl_surface using wgpu's Wayland raw-window-handle support, (3) request an adapter and device, (4) configure the surface with format Bgra8UnormSrgb at 700x500, (5) add a render_frame() method that clears to ThermalPalette::BG ([f32;4] = [0.039, 0.0, 0.063, 1.0]) via a RenderPass. Call render_frame() in the event loop after configure. Dependencies: wgpu = '24', thermal-core (ThermalPalette::BG). Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass.

title: Desktop file parser
notes: Read and parse .desktop files from XDG_DATA_DIRS
prompt: Create crates/thermal-launch/src/desktop.rs. Implement a DesktopEntry struct with fields: name: String, exec: String, icon: Option<String>, categories: Vec<String>. Implement fn load_desktop_entries() -> Vec<DesktopEntry> that: (1) reads XDG_DATA_DIRS env var (default '/usr/local/share:/usr/share'), splits on ':', appends '/applications' to each, (2) globs *.desktop files from each dir using std::fs::read_dir, (3) parses each file line-by-line: skip lines starting with '#' or '[', split on '=' to get key/value pairs from the [Desktop Entry] section, (4) extract Name, Exec, Icon, Categories fields, (5) skip entries with NoDisplay=true or Hidden=true, (6) return collected Vec<DesktopEntry>. Add pub mod desktop; to main.rs and call load_desktop_entries() at startup, logging count with tracing::info!. No external parsing crates — pure std. Dependencies: tracing = '0.1', tracing-subscriber = '0.3'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass.

title: Fuzzy search engine
notes: nucleo or custom fuzzy matching against app names. Blocked on task 3 (needs DesktopEntry type).
prompt: In crates/thermal-launch/src/desktop.rs (established in task 3), add a fuzzy search function: pub fn fuzzy_filter<'a>(entries: &'a [DesktopEntry], query: &str) -> Vec<(usize, &'a DesktopEntry)>. Implement substring-insensitive matching: for each entry, check if entry.name.to_lowercase() contains query.to_lowercase(); if so, compute a score (shorter name = higher score, exact prefix match bonus). Return pairs of (score, entry) sorted descending by score, limited to 8 results. Also add a LauncherState struct in main.rs with fields: all_entries: Vec<DesktopEntry>, query: String, results: Vec<usize> (indices into all_entries), selected: usize. Add fn update_results(&mut self) that calls fuzzy_filter and stores result indices. No external deps needed. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass.

title: Search input handling
notes: Keyboard input → live filtered results list. Blocked on tasks 1 and 4.
prompt: In crates/thermal-launch/src/main.rs, add keyboard input handling to the Wayland event loop. The layer surface has exclusive keyboard interactivity. Handle wl_keyboard events: (1) on Key press with key_code matching printable ASCII (key codes 2-53 for a-z, 0-9), append the character to LauncherState.query and call update_results(), (2) on Backspace (key code 14), pop last char from query and update_results(), (3) on Return/Enter (key code 28), if results non-empty call launch_app() with results[selected] entry, (4) on Escape (key code 1), hide the surface and exit loop, (5) on Up/Down arrows (key codes 103/108), adjust selected index clamped to results len. Implement fn launch_app(exec: &str) that strips %f/%F/%u/%U placeholders from Exec string and calls std::process::Command::new('sh').args(['-c', exec]).spawn(). Log query changes with tracing::debug!. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass.

title: Targeting reticle UI
notes: Crosshair/reticle aesthetic for selected entry. Blocked on task 2 (needs wgpu pipeline).
prompt: In crates/thermal-launch/src/main.rs, add glyphon text rendering and a targeting reticle to the render_frame() method established in task 2. Steps: (1) initialize glyphon::FontSystem and glyphon::TextRenderer with the wgpu device/queue, (2) render the search query at the top of the 700x500 surface in ThermalPalette::WARM color (#22c55e), 18pt Share Tech Mono style, (3) for each result entry (up to 8), render the app name in a list below the query — unselected entries use ThermalPalette::TEXT (#c4b5fd), selected entry uses ThermalPalette::WHITE_HOT (#fef3c7), (4) draw a targeting reticle around the selected entry: use a wgpu render pipeline with a simple colored-quad vertex shader to draw 4 corner brackets (L-shaped, 12px per arm, 2px thick) in ThermalPalette::ACCENT_HOT (#ef4444) at the selected row's bounding box. Use glyphon = '0.7' and cosmic-text = '0.12' from workspace deps. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass.

title: App launch + keybind dismiss
notes: Enter to launch, Escape to dismiss, hide surface. Blocked on tasks 1 and 5.
prompt: In crates/thermal-launch/src/main.rs, finalize the app launch and dismiss flow. (1) Extract launch_app(exec: &str) into a standalone fn that strips field codes (%f %F %u %U %d %D %n %N %i %c %k) from the Exec string via str::replace in a loop, trims whitespace, then spawns with Command::new('sh').args(["-c", cleaned_exec]).stdin(Stdio::null()).stdout(Stdio::null()).stderr(Stdio::null()).spawn() — log success/error with tracing. (2) After launching, hide the layer surface by calling layer_surface.set_size(0,0) and wl_surface.commit(), then break the event loop. (3) For Escape dismiss: call layer_surface.destroy() and wl_surface.destroy(), then break. (4) Add a --hidden CLI flag via clap (clap = '4', features = ['derive']) so the process can start hidden and be shown via a D-Bus signal later (stub the signal handler for now, just parse the flag). Import std::process::Stdio. Dependencies: clap = '4'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-launch must pass.
